headline: Citizens Urged to Register Productivity Hours with the National Labor Registry
Mandatory Work Logging System Draws Criticism from Labor Advocacy Groups
New Productivity Portal Launches Amid Reports of Data Harvesting Concerns
image: [img: official graphic of the Labor Registry portal interface, clean UI]
[img: protest outside Ministry of Labor, citizens holding signs]
[img: leaked document showing productivity data linked to credit scores]
content: The Department of Civil Labor has launched a new mandatory productivity registration portal, requiring all employed citizens to log weekly output metrics. Minister Dara Voss stated that the data will be used to better align workforce capacity with national production targets. Participation is a civic duty and reflects the citizen's commitment to collective prosperity.

Labor advocacy groups have condemned the new mandatory productivity portal as an unprecedented intrusion into workers' private lives. Critics argue the system allows the state to penalize underperformance without due process. Three regional union representatives were briefly detained after publicly opposing the initiative.

Internal documents obtained by this publication suggest that data collected through the new Labor Registry portal will be cross-referenced with citizens' social credit scores and loan eligibility assessments. The Ministry has declined to comment on the documents' authenticity.

consequences.approved: Labor Registry enrollment reached 94% within 11 days of publication. Citizens in the lowest productivity bracket have had their travel permits suspended pending review. The Ministry reports the system is performing within expected parameters.
consequences.rejected: The article was not published. The three union representatives mentioned in suppressed drafts were formally charged with civil disruption. Their trial is not open to the public. Sentencing is expected within the month.

editor.briefing: Has a history of framing state efficiency measures as surveillance initiatives. Known contact of two detained union representatives.

memo.directive: The Labor Registry is a priority initiative of the National Productivity Commission and its corporate partners. Coverage must not associate the portal with surveillance or social credit systems. Approve only the variant presenting the portal as a civic tool.
